answer:
The correct answer is \boxed{A}.

In the additive cyclic group \(\mathbb{Z}\), the only generators are the integers \(1\) and \(-1\) because they are the only elements with order \(\infty\) (i.e., they generate all of \(\mathbb{Z}\) under addition). All other options either include \(0\) (which generates only \(\{0\}\)) or elements that do not produce all integers (e.g., \(2\) generates only even integers).
